title: Refresh/maintenance process + steward handover
context: Once the catalog has a body of published records, it needs a documented refresh and maintenance process so it does not become stale after the initial contributors move on. The process covers: how often records are re-validated (freshness SLA), who is responsible (Maintainer and Steward), what triggers a re-verification (drift-017 detection of changed terms, or the scheduled refresh window expiring), how a new cohort is nominated and triaged, and how stewardship is handed over if the current Steward is no longer available. Depends on drift-017 (the technical drift detection is in place before the process is documented).
objective: Document the catalog's refresh/maintenance process (freshness SLA, re-verification triggers, new cohort nomination workflow) and the steward handover procedure, ensuring the catalog can be maintained after the initial contributors move on without silently going stale.
acceptanceCriteria: A committed maintenance process document (e.g. MAINTENANCE.md) covers: freshness SLA per source class (annual, or on drift signal), who re-verifies (Maintainer + Access/License/PII reviewer), how a re-verification is triggered (drift-017 flag or scheduled), how new cohort nominations are accepted (community PR + gate-003).
The document defines the Steward handover process: what artifacts must be transferred, what commitments must be re-confirmed, and how the new Steward is recorded in GOVERNANCE.md or equivalent.
The document defines the escalation path if no Steward is available (indexing halts; records are not silently deprecated without human decision).
The process is consistent with drift-017 outputs (termsChangedSinceSnapshot triggers a human re-verification, not an automated update).
output: A committed MAINTENANCE.md (or equivalent) documenting the refresh process, freshness SLAs, re-verification triggers, new cohort nomination workflow, and steward handover process.
